summary: Cantrill reflects on how a chance reading of a Byte magazine article about QNX in 1993 led to formative summer internships at the company, and announces that DTrace is being ported to QNX. The post weaves together personal history, the announcement of the port, and a tribute to the late Dan Hildebrand, who mentored Cantrill during his time at QNX. The announcement serves as both a professional milestone and a bittersweet reminder of mentors lost.
key_points: A Byte magazine article about QNX inspired Cantrill to cold-email for a summer job, which he got
DTrace is being ported to QNX by Colin Burgess and Thomas Fletcher
The port was the mystery 'other system' Cantrill alluded to in his Google talk
Dan Hildebrand, who gave Cantrill his start, passed away and is remembered fondly
money_quotes: There are moments in anyone's life that seem mundane at the time, but become pivotal in retrospect: that chance meeting of your spouse, or the job you applied for on a lark, or the inspirational course that you just stumbled upon.
Some speculated that this 'surely' must be Windows – so hopefully the fact that it was QNX will serve to remind that it's a big heterogeneous world out there.
I would love nothing more right now than to call up danh, chat about the exciting prospects for DTrace on QNX, and reminisce about the many conversations over our shared cube wall so long ago.
key_insight: Pivotal career moments often emerge from seemingly mundane chances taken—a magazine read in an airport, a cold email sent on a whim—and the mentors who respond to those chances leave lasting marks even after they're gone.
